title: Decide RepoBrief package and repository rename path
goal: Document whether repo, package, imports and CLI remain lenskit/RepoBrief split or move through staged rename

acceptance.assertion: The slice is implemented or explicitly closed against current main/PR state with no hidden broadening.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: RepoBrief boundaries are preserved: no hidden refresh, no Git mutation in RepoBrief core, no review/runtime correctness claim.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: Relevant tests/checks are run, or a precise blocker/gap is recorded.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: The output states what the evidence does not establish.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

validation: Lenskit PR #988 merged with all required GitHub checks passing, including pytest-full, browser-tests, release-candidate, Ruff and CodeQL.
Current Lenskit main ac7d35ff987a706e0dde0e042a34873d496c91b2 still contains the decision artifacts unchanged.
Focused current-main validation: 4 identity/distribution decision tests passed.
metadata.outcome: Closed as already implemented on current Lenskit main. RepoBrief remains the product and primary CLI name; repository lenskit and Python namespace merger.lenskit remain stable for the 2.x line. Any later rename requires an intentional breaking major release with consumer inventory, migration, deprecation and rollback evidence.

verified_acceptance.rbv1-t018-scope-bound: No new Lenskit mutation was created. Existing PR #988 was read back and current main was revalidated.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t018-boundary-preserved: The decision changes naming policy only and explicitly preserves RepoBrief read-only and non-authoritative boundaries.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t018-tests-or-gap: Four focused current-main tests passed and all historical PR #988 required checks are green.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t018-non-claims: The evidence does not establish unknown-consumer absence, public package readiness, runtime correctness beyond tested surfaces, or permanent prohibition of a future breaking-major rename.
